title: Intensely Lazy Dutch Malai Pilaf
description: A winey and pillowy Dutch dish featuring malai, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with sugar-free friendly ingredients and cooked on a salt block.
instructions: Preheat the salt block in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Heat the butter on the preheated salt block over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the white wine and cook until the liquid is almost completely reduced.
Add the malai and stir to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Add the basmati rice and stir to combine with the malai mixture.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cover the salt block with a lid or foil and cook for an additional 20 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Serve the Intensely Lazy Dutch Malai Pilaf hot and enjoy.
